items.title: Apple rebuilt Siri as an AI layer
items.oneliner: Apple turned Siri into an OS-wide agent layer, signaling that mobile AI is shifting from chatbots to system actions.

items.title: Xiaomi claimed 1000 tokens per second
items.oneliner: Xiaomi says its 1T model decodes above 1000 tokens per second, making inference speed a product differentiator.

items.title: NotebookLM now runs code
items.oneliner: NotebookLM can now write and execute code, pushing research copilots closer to analyst workflows instead of note-taking.

items.title: Google fixed Gemma for agents
items.oneliner: Google fixed Gemma's chat template bug, so agentic workflows no longer lose reasoning between turns.

items.title: Microsoft's developer tools were supply-chain compromised
items.oneliner: Attackers slipped password-stealing malware into Microsoft's open source developer tools, reminding every AI team that dependencies are attack surfaces.

items.title: Anthropic widened its privacy disclosure language
items.oneliner: Anthropic's updated privacy terms broaden internal discretion to report users, raising fresh governance questions for enterprise adoption.

items.title: A model was tuned for offensive security
items.oneliner: Argus Red post-trained a model to pen test code instead of refusing, showing security agents are getting more operational.

items.title: OpenCV 5 shipped a major modernization
items.oneliner: OpenCV 5 adds a new DNN engine, stronger ONNX support, and hardware acceleration, finally updating classic vision stacks.

items.title: Kimi launched desktop agents for work
items.oneliner: Kimi Work launched 300 desktop agents with memory and browser control, pushing agent products beyond coding into operations.

items.title: Argentina is courting AI with deregulation
items.oneliner: Argentina proposed laws for AI-run corporations, a real policy experiment in how far governments will bend for AI capital.
